jokes.text: Charlie struggling to put his toes in five-fingered shoes, can't tell where his pinky is
jokes.setup: Charlie attempting to put on specialized footwear
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to manage basic shoe-wearing despite the shoes being designed for feet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his childlike confusion over basic tasks. Physical comedy that establishes character immediately.

dialogue.line: Charlie struggling to put his toes in five-fingered shoes, can't tell where his pinky is

jokes.text: Charlie got five-finger shoes because Dennis yells at him for taking shoes off in laser tag, wanted shoes 'more like bare feet'
jokes.setup: Logical explanation for shoe choice
jokes.punchline: The solution makes no logical sense and defeats the purpose
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic - Charlie's problem-solving that creates bigger problems. Shows his warped reasoning perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I got five-finger shoes because Dennis yells at me for taking my shoes off in laser tag, and I wanted shoes that were more like bare feet

jokes.text: Dennis explains Charlie takes shoes off, doesn't hold gun, plus cheese oozes out of his feet with awful smell
jokes.setup: Reasonable complaint about Charlie's behavior
jokes.punchline: The disgusting detail about cheese oozing from his feet
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross Charlie detail. The casual mention of cheese storage in shoes is wonderfully disgusting and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Charlie takes shoes off, doesn't hold gun, plus cheese oozes out of his feet with awful smell

jokes.text: Charlie reveals he hides cheese in his shoes, can't fit cheese in the five-finger shoes
jokes.setup: Previous mention of cheese in shoes
jokes.punchline: Charlie treats shoe-cheese storage as a serious logistical problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - not only does Charlie store cheese in shoes, he's genuinely frustrated by storage limitations. Pure Charlie absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I hide cheese in my shoes, okay? But I can't fit cheese in the five-finger shoes.

jokes.text: Charlie saying he's 'kind of sick and tired of guarding the base' and wanting to 'go out there and have some fun'
jokes.setup: Charlie in his usual role
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of Charlie expressing actual desires and dissatisfaction
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment - Charlie rarely expresses wants this clearly. Sets up his arc for the episode.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm kind of sick and tired of guarding the base. I want to go out there and have some fun.

jokes.text: Charlie's 'riddle': Why do blind people hate skydiving? Because it scares the hell out of their dogs
jokes.setup: Charlie offers to tell riddles, sets up blind people/skydiving premise
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical answer about guide dogs
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie doesn't understand what riddles are, but the actual joke structure is weak.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Why do blind people hate skydiving? Because it scares the hell out of their dogs

jokes.text: Dennis: 'That's not a riddle' Charlie: 'Yeah, it's a riddle about blind people and their dogs' Dennis: 'That's a joke' Charlie: 'Jokes are funny and that wasn't funny'
jokes.setup: Dennis correcting Charlie about what constitutes a riddle vs joke
jokes.punchline: Charlie accidentally admits his own joke wasn't funny
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware moment where Charlie accidentally critiques his own bad joke. Great economy and character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: 'That's not a riddle'

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'Yeah, it's a riddle about blind people and their dogs'

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: 'That's a joke'

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'Jokes are funny and that wasn't funny'

jokes.text: Mac and Dee tumbling out of air ducts, fighting and covered in dust
jokes.setup: Professional laser tag strategy involving air duct sniping
jokes.punchline: Complete physical failure and sibling-like fighting
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy, works for the moment but not particularly clever or character-specific.

dialogue.line: Mac and Dee tumbling out of air ducts, fighting and covered in dust

jokes.text: Dee: 'I spit in your mouth after you jammed me in the tit with your scrawny little elbow' Mac: 'My elbows are massive and you have no tits!'
jokes.setup: Explaining their air duct altercation
jokes.punchline: Mac's delusional body image and cruel but accurate assessment of Dee
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Mac's body dysmorphia and the gang's casual cruelty to Dee. Well-integrated insults.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I spit in your mouth after you jammed me in the tit with your scrawny little elbow

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: My elbows are massive and you have no tits!

jokes.text: Dennis's complete tone shift praising Dee
jokes.setup: Dennis just screamed at them
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to overly effusive praise for Dee
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's manipulation tactics, the whiplash tone change is unsettling and funny

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dennis's complete tone shift praising Dee

jokes.text: Dennis showing 'Fun Zone dolla dolla bills' and saying 'I'm trying to get that paper, son'
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining their motivation for laser tag excellence
jokes.punchline: Treating arcade tickets like serious money and adopting street slang
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's delusions of coolness and how seriously he takes meaningless achievements.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm trying to get that paper, son

jokes.text: Dennis explaining his psychological manipulation: withholding compliments from Mac makes him angry and subservient, showering Dee with compliments makes her angry at herself
jokes.setup: Charlie asking why Dennis treats Mac and Dee differently
jokes.punchline: Dennis's calculated psychological abuse disguised as strategy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing Dennis's manipulation. Perfect character integration - only Dennis would strategize emotional abuse.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Why do you treat Mac and Dee so differently?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's simple psychology. I withhold compliments from Mac, which makes him angry and subservient. I shower Dee with compliments, which makes her angry at herself.

jokes.text: Charlie: 'It's another riddle' about Dennis's psychological explanation
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining complex psychological manipulation
jokes.punchline: Charlie thinks it's a riddle because he doesn't understand it
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Charlie's consistent inability to process complex information. Solid character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'It's another riddle'

jokes.text: Charlie making 'pew pew' sounds with his laser tag gun while talking to Dennis
jokes.setup: Charlie returning from successful laser tag run
jokes.punchline: He's so excited he can't stop making shooting sounds like a child
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Charlie's childlike enthusiasm. Not groundbreaking but fits the character.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Pew pew pew pew pew!

jokes.text: Charlie's riddle: 'Is a fart a ghost?' Answer: 'Yeah. It's the ghost of lunch'
jokes.setup: Charlie posing another 'riddle'
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical but oddly poetic answer about farts being lunch ghosts
jokes.explanation: Genuinely creative and weird in a way that feels authentic to Charlie. Unexpectedly poetic phrasing makes it memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Is a fart a ghost?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah. It's the ghost of lunch

jokes.text: Dennis and Charlie reveal they turn Charlie's pack off so the team doesn't lose points when he gets shot
jokes.setup: Charlie asking about his pack not being on
jokes.punchline: The revelation that they've been lying to him about participating
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how the gang treats Charlie. Sets up the bigger revelation about his role.

dialogue.line: Dennis and Charlie reveal they turn Charlie's pack off so the team doesn't lose points when he gets shot

jokes.text: Charlie discovers the gun sounds are him making 'pew pew' noises, not the gun
jokes.setup: Charlie claiming his gun makes noise
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's been making the sounds himself the whole time
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to earlier setup. Shows Charlie's childlike delusion and the gang's manipulation perfectly.

jokes.text: Dennis explains Charlie is 'an amazing decoy' who 'adds a monster element that distracts and frightens but entertains the kids'
jokes.setup: Trying to soften the blow about Charlie's real role
jokes.punchline: Describing Charlie as literally a monster that frightens children
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific character moment. Dennis's attempt at kindness is actually more insulting than honesty would be.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: an amazing decoy

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: adds a monster element that distracts and frightens but entertains the kids

jokes.text: Dennis compares Charlie to Cookie Monster, then realizes 'there's no cookies' and 'kids are scared of you and not really scared of him'
jokes.setup: Trying to make Charlie feel better with Cookie Monster comparison
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizing the comparison makes no sense and is actually worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dennis trying to help but making things worse. Shows his inability to be genuinely comforting.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're like Cookie Monster

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Wait, there's no cookies

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Kids are scared of you and not really scared of him

jokes.text: Charlie happily making laser tag sounds when his pack is turned on for the first time
jokes.setup: Charlie's pack being activated after learning the truth
jokes.punchline: His pure joy at finally experiencing real laser tag sounds
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing Charlie's childlike wonder. Good payoff to the sound effects setup.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie happily making laser tag sounds when his pack is turned on for the first time

jokes.text: Charlie thinking 'what Big Mo looks like' is a riddle
jokes.setup: Dennis wonders what Big Mo looks like
jokes.punchline: Charlie thinks this is another riddle
jokes.explanation: Consistent callback to Charlie's misunderstanding of riddles

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: what Big Mo looks like' is a riddle

jokes.text: Charlie's riddle answer: 'Cantaloupes' because 'if you're holding a pile of cantaloupes, you're not gonna be able to see very much'
jokes.setup: Dennis poses actual riddle about darkness
jokes.punchline: Charlie's literal and completely wrong interpretation involving fruit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie logic - technically correct but completely misses the point. The cantaloupe visualization is absurdly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Cantaloupes because if you're holding a pile of cantaloupes, you're not gonna be able to see very much

jokes.text: Charlie: 'You telling me you can see through a pile of cantaloupes?'
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining the correct answer is 'darkness'
jokes.punchline: Charlie doubling down on cantaloupe logic
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Charlie's stubborn commitment to his wrong answer. Perfect character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'You telling me you can see through a pile of cantaloupes?'

jokes.text: Mac and Dee went to the 'Fun Zone instead of the Maintenance Zone in the ceiling'
jokes.setup: Explaining why they're suddenly having fun
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on 'Fun Zone' vs maintenance area
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that doesn't really land. Characters acknowledge it's lame but that doesn't make it funnier.

dialogue.line: Mac and Dee went to the 'Fun Zone instead of the Maintenance Zone in the ceiling'

jokes.text: Charlie: 'That's not a joke, that's a riddle. And don't steal my shit, you stupid bird-faced bitch!'
jokes.setup: Dee telling the Fun Zone joke
jokes.punchline: Charlie defending his territory and calling Dee a bird
jokes.explanation: Good combination of Charlie's riddle obsession and standard Dee abuse. Shows character relationships well.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That's not a joke, that's a riddle. And don't steal my shit, you stupid bird-faced bitch!

jokes.text: Mac: 'Rutherford B. Crazy, of course' when asked about the $100 bill
jokes.setup: Dennis asking who's on the hundred dollar Fun Zone bill
jokes.punchline: Mac's confident but completely wrong answer about a made-up person
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's tendency to confidently state nonsense. Sets up the Rutherford mythology.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: 'Rutherford B. Crazy, of course' when asked about the $100 bill

jokes.text: Dennis creates elaborate backstory about Rutherford B. Crazy starting in laser beam business and never giving up on fundamentals
jokes.setup: Building on Mac's made-up character
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating arcade mascot as serious business inspiration
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's need to be the authority on everything, even made-up arcade lore. A bit verbose for the payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Rutherford B. Crazy started in the laser beam business and never gave up on the fundamentals

jokes.text: Dennis's motivational speech about Rutherford's laser beam business
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to explain the lesson of Rutherford
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-philosophical business wisdom about laser beams
jokes.explanation: Dennis's delusional leadership style, the laser beam focus is absurd

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dennis's motivational speech about Rutherford's laser beam business

jokes.text: Mac and Charlie: 'I don't think Rutherford be a real person. That's a cartoon.'
jokes.setup: Dennis's elaborate Rutherford backstory
jokes.punchline: Casual observation that they're talking about a cartoon mascot
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Dennis's pretentious speech. Simple but effective reality check.

dialogue.speaker: Mac and Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't think Rutherford be a real person. That's a cartoon.

jokes.text: Frank created a diversion by telling one dad another dad said his kid had a 'tight little dumper'
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his laser tag strategy
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment that would obviously cause a fight
jokes.explanation: Dark and inappropriate in Frank's typical style. Shows his complete lack of boundaries.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: created a diversion by telling one dad another dad said his kid had a 'tight little dumper'

jokes.text: Frank went to 'Wing Zone' instead of 'war zone' because of the line
jokes.setup: Confusion about where Frank was
jokes.punchline: Frank prioritizing food over strategy
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that works for Frank's character - his priorities are always off.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Frank went to 'Wing Zone' instead of 'war zone' because of the line

jokes.text: Charlie shooting Frank with laser gun when wings arrive, Frank's excited reaction
jokes.setup: Frank bringing wings, Charlie finally having working gun
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately shooting Frank, Frank enjoying it
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy that works but isn't particularly sophisticated. Good character beat.

jokes.text: Dennis explaining Charlie will lose his 'little plastic army guys' and 'box of bouncy balls' if they stop winning
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to motivate Charlie to keep playing
jokes.punchline: Treating children's toys as serious stakes
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's manipulation and Charlie's childlike desires. Good character work for both.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dennis explaining Charlie will lose his 'little plastic army guys' and 'box of bouncy balls' if they stop winning

jokes.text: Charlie explains he eats bouncy balls to 'test them in my stomach to see how they pass'
jokes.setup: Dennis catching Charlie eating arcade prizes
jokes.punchline: Charlie's scientific justification for eating inedible objects
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - disgusting behavior with pseudo-scientific rationalization. Uniquely disturbing and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I eat bouncy balls to 'test them in my stomach to see how they pass'

jokes.text: Dennis's speech about being 'a ghost, like the fart of meals past'
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining what happens when they stop playing
jokes.punchline: Combining ghost metaphor with Charlie's earlier fart riddle
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that elevates Charlie's silly riddle into Dennis's existential crisis. Good writing technique.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: a ghost, like the fart of meals past

jokes.text: Frank reveals Rutherford B. Crazy's real name was Larry Takashi and he worked with him in the '90s
jokes.setup: Group's elaborate mythology around Rutherford B. Crazy
jokes.punchline: Frank casually knows the real boring truth
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of the built-up mythology. Frank's casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Rutherford B. Crazy's real name was Larry Takashi and he worked with him in the '90s

jokes.text: Larry Takashi threw himself down a wishing well with suicide note saying 'It all meant nothing'
jokes.setup: Learning about the laser tag founder's fate
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dramatic suicide method with devastatingly simple note
jokes.explanation: Dark and absurd in perfect balance. The wishing well detail is wonderfully ridiculous while the note is genuinely bleak.

dialogue.line: Larry Takashi threw himself down a wishing well with suicide note saying 'It all meant nothing'

jokes.text: The gang's fake-out ending: pretending to leave then attacking Big Mo saying 'We ain't going anywhere. We're never leaving, you little piece of shit.'
jokes.setup: Touching moment of growth where they decide to move on
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverting to their worst selves and attacking a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sunny ending - subverts any character growth and shows their true nature. Excellent structure and character consistency.

dialogue.line: We ain't going anywhere. We're never leaving, you little piece of shit.

jokes.text: 'You thought we'd gone? We ain't going anywhere'
jokes.setup: The supposed character growth and enlightenment
jokes.punchline: Dennis menacing a child with psychotic intensity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character consistency - Dennis's inability to change, now with murderous undertones

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You thought we'd gone? We ain't going anywhere
